comments.text: Ma and I wrote a short note on this problem, showing that it can be reformulated as a finite-family Turán problem. More precisely, for each fixed $k$ there exists a finite family of graphs $\mathcal F_k^{\min}$ (depending only on $k$) such that\[ f(n,k)=\binom{n}{2}-\mathrm{ex}\bigl(n,\mathcal F_k^{\min}\bigr). \]In particular, for $k=1,2,3$ we obtain\[ f(n,1)=\binom{n}{2}-\mathrm{ex}(n,K_3)=\binom{n}{2}-\left\lfloor\frac{n^2}{4}\right\rfloor,\]\[ f(n,2)=\binom{n}{2}-\mathrm{ex}(n,C_4),\]and\[ f(n,3)=\binom{n}{2}-\mathrm{ex}\bigl(n,\{C_5,\,K_{2,3},\,K_1\vee 2K_2\}\bigr),\]where $K_1\vee 2K_2$ denotes the join of a single vertex with two disjoint edges. We do not know whether this Turán-type reformulation is already what Erdős had in mind as an ''answer'' for $f(n,k)$.
